## Summary

Overhauls the Threat/Antagonist/Ending layer renderer to use a literary pressure taxonomy instead of commercial-plot labels. Eliminates raw JSON from the author-facing UI.

**Key changes:**
- **Renamed "Named Antagonists" → "Pressure Agents"** with inferred literary pressure types: maternal obligation, marital constraint, social convention, sexual destabilizer, symbolic terminal force, medical-social surveillance, narrative pressure
- **Terminal Ledger**: Raw JSON replaced with plain-English character cards showing name, final belief state, last-seen location, and closure status. Collapsed by default.
- **Ending Accountability**: Background/foil figures (lady in black, unnamed lovers, two young lovers, etc.) filtered out — these serve symbolic/atmospheric functions, not open narrative promises. Collapsed by default.
- **Layer title**: "Threat · Antagonist · Ending" → "Threat · Pressure · Ending"
- **LABEL_HINTS**: Tooltip definitions added for all new pressure vocabulary

## Scope

**Touches:** `components/ledger/StoryLedgerLayers.tsx` — renderer only
**Does NOT touch:** Extraction prompts, pipeline, API routes, or data schema

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

Renderer-only change. No data model or pipeline changes. Existing artifacts render correctly with the new component — the pressure type inference runs client-side from character names.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — renderer-only, no extraction or pipeline code modified
